title: QUIC Hosted Interop-Runner Handshake Corroboration Architecture
purpose: Describe the manual hosted workflow boundary for collecting one narrow interop-runner handshake artifact bundle without changing ordinary CI gates or public support claims.
design_summary: The hosted lane is a `workflow_dispatch`-only GitHub Actions workflow. It checks out `quic-dotnet` and a separate `quic-interop-runner` workspace, verifies that Docker and Python are available, then runs the repo-local helper in server-role mode for the single `handshake` testcase against the established `quic-go` client slot. The helper remains responsible for image build, runner invocation, report capture, and evidence-backed advisory classification. The workflow always uploads the `artifacts/interop-runner/` tree so a failed or advisory runner outcome still leaves the JSON report, Markdown report, stderr, invocation summary, build log, qlogs, pcaps, and output logs available for review.
alternatives_considered: Add the interop runner invocation to ordinary push or pull-request CI. Rejected because the current evidence is advisory and should not block the green local build/test/package baseline.
Create a broad hosted matrix across roles, peers, and testcases. Rejected because the first hosted lane should corroborate the smallest already-supported helper cell before expanding scope.
Treat a hosted artifact upload as a public interop-readiness proof. Rejected because the lane is explicitly advisory and depends on preserved evidence inspection.
edge_cases_and_constraints: The runner checkout may fail or its CLI may drift independently of this repository.
The workflow must upload artifacts on both success and failure so diagnostic evidence is not lost.
Manual dispatch keeps the lane outside push, pull-request, build, test, and package gates.
The workflow must not rewrite the runner registry or imply Incursa.Quic has an upstream interop-runner implementation entry.
The helper's advisory classification must remain narrow and evidence-backed; the runner's own failed report remains part of the audit bundle when its post-check fails.
data_and_state_considerations: Hosted runs produce transient GitHub Actions artifacts containing the repo-local `artifacts/interop-runner/` tree. They do not commit generated reports, modify the runner checkout, or persist transport state beyond the uploaded artifact bundle.
risks: Hosted Docker, Python, or runner dependency drift can fail the manual workflow even when the repo-local baseline is green.
A manual workflow badge or successful dispatch could be overread as broad interop readiness unless the advisory boundary remains explicit.
Uploading the whole artifact tree can be large if the lane is widened without a separate requirement and proof review.

supplemental_sections.heading: Scope
supplemental_sections.content: This design covers only the manual hosted `handshake` corroboration lane. It does not change protocol runtime behavior, the public API surface, ordinary CI gating, or the external runner repository.
